commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an xAI stream wrapper helper that appends `include: ["reasoning.encrypted_content"]` for reasoning-capable xAI Responses models, plus focused tests and xAI docs wording.

PR surface: Source +25, Tests +49, Docs +4. Total +78 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level payload generation: current main drops reasoning controls for `supportsReasoningEffort: false` xAI models and only adds encrypted include when an effort payload survives. No live xAI reproduction or after-fix runtime proof was provided.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Contributor proof or maintainer acceptance of the provider compatibility risk is needed; there is no narrow code repair identified from this review.

target.title: fix(xai): request encrypted reasoning include for all reasoning models
target.body:
<!--
Optional linked context:
Add a visible `Closes #<issue-number>` or `Related: #<issue-number>` line
below this comment.

Required PR title:
type: user-facing description
Use a parenthesized scope only when it adds clarity:
fix(auth): login redirect loops when session cookie is expired

Types: feat, fix, improve, refactor, docs, chore.
For fixes, describe the user-visible symptom and trigger:
fix: task list fails to load when user has no environments
Avoid implementation details such as:
fix: add null check to task query
-->

## What Problem This Solves

Most of the grok models are actually reasoning models, and the current implementation only hard-coded to include encrypted_content for `grok-4.3*` model and send the encypted_content back to the server in the next turn request.

## Why This Change Was Made

<!--
In one or two sentences, explain the complete shipped solution, key design
decisions, and relevant boundaries or non-goals. Include implementation detail
only when it helps reviewers understand user-visible behavior or risk.
Avoid file-by-file narration.
-->

The encrypted_content behavior is only controlled by whether the model support reasoning_effort instead of whether the model is reasoning.

This PR will always ask for the encrypted_content to be returned and be sent back in the next turn request.

## User Impact

This will improve the quality of the model output for other non-4.3 grok models.

## Evidence

<!--
Show the most useful proof that this change works. Screenshots, screencasts,
terminal output, focused tests, CI results, live observations, redacted logs,
and artifact links are all useful. Include before/after evidence for visual
changes when it clarifies the result.

Reviewers will inspect the code, tests, and CI. Use this section to make the
validation easy to understand, not to restate the diff.
-->

I dumped the request body, and saw no encrypted_content being included, and the code does show encrypted_content is only being used for `grok-4.3*` model.
target.author: geraint0923
